nodes.x: Praise ye the Lord. Praise ye the Lord from the heavens: praise him in the heights.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise, O ye servants of the Lord, praise the name of the Lord.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise ye the name of the Lord; praise him, O ye servants of the Lord.
Praise him, ye heavens of heavens, and ye waters that be above the heavens.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
Praise ye him, all his angels: praise ye him, all his hosts.
Bless ye the Lord, all ye his hosts; ye ministers of his, that do his pleasure.
O praise the Lord, all ye nations: praise him, all ye people.
Praise him for his mighty acts: praise him according to his excellent greatness.
He also exalteth the horn of his people, the praise of all his saints; even of the children of Israel, a people near unto him. Praise ye the Lord.
Praise ye him, sun and moon: praise him, all ye stars of light.
And so great shall be the glory of his presence that the sun shall hide his face in shame, and the moon shall withhold its light, and the stars shall be hurled from their places.
The sun shall be no more thy light by day; neither for brightness shall the moon give light unto thee: but the Lord shall be unto thee an everlasting light, and thy God thy glory.

May the celestial light, reflected by our little Millennial Morning “ Star,” continue to shine with increasing brilliancy, until the perfect day, when it will be swallowed up, and the whole Earth illuminated, by the untold splendour of the SUN OF RIGHTEOUSNESS : and may the Holy Spirit of God ever dwell in us all richly, and qualify us to honourably fulfil our respective duties in the name of of…
Praise ye him, all his angels: praise ye him, all his hosts.
Praise him, ye heavens of heavens, and ye waters that be above the heavens.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise ye the Lord from the heavens: praise him in the heights.
Let the heaven and earth praise him, the seas, and every thing that moveth therein.
Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters.

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.
And worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters—
Let them praise the name of the Lord: for he commanded, and they were created.
Let them praise the name of the Lord: for his name alone is excellent; his glory is above the earth and heaven.
And then began these men to call upon the name of the Lord, and the Lord blessed them;
Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the Lord be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.
Let them speak of his mercy and his goodness — let therti proclaim his wisdom and his power, in delivering them from the hands of their enemies.
He hath also stablished them for ever and ever: he hath made a decree which shall not pass.
All his transgressions that he hath committed, they shall not be mentioned unto him: in his righteousness that he hath done he shall live.
None of his sins that he hath committed shall be mentioned unto him: he hath done that which is lawful and right; he shall surely live.
For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.
Nevertheless he that standeth steadfast in his heart, having no necessity, but hath power over his own will, and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keep his virgin, doeth well.
And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.
Praise the Lord from the earth, ye dragons, and all deeps:
All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord, when they hear the words of thy mouth.
Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:
For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
Fire, and hail; snow, and vapour; stormy wind fulfilling his word:

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens; and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth: he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.
For he commandeth, and raiseth the stormy wind, which lifteth up the waves thereof.
He sendeth out his word, and melteth them: he causeth his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
He causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain; he bringeth the wind out of his treasuries.
Mountains, and all hills; fruitful trees, and all cedars:

Our road to-day led through a pass in the mountains by a gradual
assent for about three miles, and then down very gradually for ten
miles among hills, plains and little vales, more rich in bunch
grass and cedar fuel, and more varied and beautiful than any other
country I ever beheld. Every high hill, every dell, every vale or
nook seemed richly coated with a living green of rich grass, and
set…
The hills were covered with the shadow of it, and the boughs thereof were like the goodly cedars.
And upon all the cedars of Lebanon, that are high and lifted up, and upon all the oaks of Bashan,
Beasts, and all cattle; creeping things, and flying fowl:
Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.

They, and every beast after his kind, and all the cattle after their kind, and every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind, and every fowl after his kind, every bird of every sort.
Of clean beasts, and of beasts that are not clean, and of fowls, and of every thing that creepeth upon the earth,
I know all the fowls of the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field are mine.
Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
Kings of the earth, and all people; princes, and all judges of the earth:
By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth.
Remember the kings, the princes, the nobles, and the great ones of the earth, and all people, and the churches, all the poor, the needy, and afflicted ones of the earth;
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
The mighty man, and the man of war, the judge, and the prophet, and the prudent, and the ancient,

h  See  mention  throughout  the  books  of  Judges,  1  and  2  Samuel,  1  and  2 
Kings,   and    references    therein.
Both young men, and maidens; old men, and children:
The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.
Children’s children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.
Son of man, there were two women, the daughters of one mother:
And when these men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them, the sons of men saw that those daughters were fair, and they took them wives, even as they chose.
All these had taken strange wives: and some of them had wives by whom they had children.
Let them praise the name of the Lord: for his name alone is excellent; his glory is above the earth and heaven.
Let them praise the name of the Lord: for he commanded, and they were created.
Let them praise thy great and terrible name; for it is holy.
Praise the Lord; for the Lord is good: sing praises unto his name; for it is pleasant.
Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name; worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness.
O Lord our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
He also exalteth the horn of his people, the praise of all his saints; even of the children of Israel, a people near unto him. Praise ye the Lord.

Thus saith the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to him whom man despiseth, to him whom the nations abhorreth, to servant of rulers: Kings shall see and arise, princes also shall worship, because of the Lord that is faithful.

Thus saith the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel, and his Holy One, to him whom man despiseth, to him whom the nation abhorreth, to a servant of rulers, Kings shall see and arise, princes also shall worship, because of the Lord that is faithful, and the Holy One of Israel, and he shall choose thee.
Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,
And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw it, gave praise unto God.
